title: RH-EML Conjecture: Numerical & Analytic Assault on the Riemann Hypothesis

key_theorem.statement: The Riemann Hypothesis is equivalent to: all non-trivial zeros of ζ(s) are EML-3 objects (lying on the EML-3 critical line Re(s)=1/2). Any off-line zero would require EML-∞ cancellation and is therefore forbidden if the EML-3 structure of ζ is self-consistent. N(T) = (T/2π)ln(T/2πe) [EML-2] + S(T) [EML-3]: depth matches RH prediction.

zero_statistics.GUE_conjecture: Zero gaps follow GUE (Gaussian Unitary Ensemble) statistics — EML-3 (random matrix eigenvalues = EML-3)

montgomery_pair_correlation.reason: sin² term = EML-3; this is the pair correlation of EML-3 zeros

eml_structure_critical_line.expression: Σ n^{-1/2}·exp(-it·ln n): superposition of EML-3 oscillations
eml_structure_critical_line.reason: exp(-it·ln n) = EML-3 (exp of imaginary); sum of EML-3 = EML-3

eml_structure_critical_line.expression: Σ n^{-σ}·exp(-it·ln n) = 0: requires deep cancellation
eml_structure_critical_line.reason: Perfect cancellation of EML-3 series requires infinite precision → EML-∞ condition
s = -2n: integer points = EML-0 locations; functional equation EML-2

zero_density_estimates.eml_rh_equivalence: RH ↔ N(σ,T) = 0 for all σ > 1/2 and all T. Under RH: off-line zero density is identically EML-0 (constant 0). Without RH: off-line density T^{2(1-σ)} is EML-2. The RH collapses an EML-2 density to EML-0 — a depth reduction.

conjecture.standard_RH: All non-trivial zeros of ζ(s) have Re(s) = 1/2
conjecture.eml_restatement: Let N(σ,T) = #{ρ: Re(ρ)>σ, |Im(ρ)|≤T}. RH ↔ N(σ,T) ≡ 0 for σ > 1/2 for all T ↔ the zero locus of ζ is a subset of the EML-3 manifold Re(s) = 1/2 ↔ any zero off the critical line would have EML-∞ depth (requiring EML-∞ cancellation). Therefore: RH ↔ all non-trivial zeros are EML-3 (not EML-∞).
evidence: All ~10^13 verified zeros lie on σ=1/2 (EML-3 location)
Zero spacing follows GUE (EML-3 random matrix statistics)
Lindelöf conjecture: ζ(1/2+it) = O(t^ε) → EML-3 boundary behavior

partial_results.result: Hardy (1914): infinitely many zeros on σ=1/2

partial_results.eml_implication: At least 1/3 of zeros provably EML-3

rh_eml_conjecture.rabbit_hole: The RH-EML restatement suggests a new approach: show that EML-∞ cancellation is impossible on the critical line by a depth-reduction argument. This connects to the GUE/random matrix approach (Keating-Snaith): the zero statistics being EML-3 (modular/spectral) is precisely what RH predicts.

eml_depth_summary.EML-2: N(T) main term (T/2π)ln(T/2πe); density exponent T^{A(σ)}; trivial zero locations
eml_depth_summary.EML-3: ζ(1/2+it) as superposition of exp(-it·ln n); S(T) = arg ζ; GUE zero spacing; non-trivial zeros (if RH true)
eml_depth_summary.EML-\u221e: Hypothetical off-line zeros requiring EML-∞ cancellation; full ζ(s) with movable zeros

rabbit_hole_log: RH-EML: The critical line σ=1/2 is distinguished as the unique line where ζ is EML-3 — both input and output have this depth. For σ≠1/2, the partial sums have EML-3 structure but the zero condition (sum=0) would require EML-∞ precision.
GUE connection: Montgomery's pair correlation = 1-(sinπu/πu)² is itself EML-3. The zeros of an EML-3 process follow EML-3 statistics — internal consistency of the EML-3 class.
Density collapse: RH collapses N(σ,T) from EML-2 (for σ>1/2) to EML-0 (identically 0). This is the most dramatic depth reduction in all of mathematics.

connections.to_session_87: Session 87: j-function = EML-3 modular. Session 89: ζ zeros = EML-3 (both are EML-3 spectral structures)
connections.to_session_69: Session 69: computable reals = EML-finite. Session 89: RH zeros (if off-line) would be EML-∞ — incomputable locations
